hawkish_phrases: Domestic inflationary pressures have continued to build
Annual growth in regular pay has risen to 3¼%, stronger than anticipated
a margin of excess demand was expected to emerge
an ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period, at a gradual pace and to a limited extent, would be appropriate
dovish_phrases: the near-term outlook for global growth has softened and downside risks to growth have increased
UK CPI inflation is likely to fall below 2% in coming months
Business investment has fallen for each of the past three quarters
The monetary policy response to Brexit, whatever form it takes, will not be automatic and could be in either direction
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, maintaining that ongoing gradual tightening would be appropriate in the baseline scenario despite intensified Brexit uncertainties and near-term growth softening.
